text.title: Fine-Tuning LoRA: Production Pipeline and Gotchas
text.summary: Agentic training pipeline, model merging, inference serving, rollback strategy, and common failure modes.

rule.statement: Implement the training pipeline as: validate data → submit training job → poll status → run eval gate → merge adapter → deploy. Never merge an adapter that has not passed the eval gate. Never skip data validation before submission.
rule.rationale: Automated pipelines without validation silently train on malformed data, consuming GPU hours and producing unusable models. The eval gate must run before merging because merged weights cannot be easily separated — a failed merge requires re-training from the base model.

example.code:
def run_lora_pipeline(dataset_path: str, output_dir: str) -> str: # 1. Validate data before GPU time validate_jsonl(dataset_path)
# 2. Train trainer = build_trainer(dataset_path, output_dir) trainer.train()
# 3. Eval gate: fail fast before merge base_scores = load_base_scores() eval_gate(trainer.model, trainer.tokenizer, domain_eval=DOMAIN_EVAL, general_eval=GENERAL_EVAL, base_scores=base_scores)
# 4. Merge adapter into base model merged_model = trainer.model.merge_and_unload() merged_model.save_pretrained(f"{output_dir}/merged")
# 5. Return merged path for serving return f"{output_dir}/merged"

rule.statement: Call merge_and_unload() to fold LoRA weights into the base model before serving. Do not serve with the adapter loaded on top of the base — it adds 10-20ms overhead per token and requires shipping two artifacts.
rule.rationale: Serving with an unmerged adapter requires loading both the base model and the adapter, adding memory overhead and latency per forward pass. After merging, the model is a single artifact with the same size as the base model and no inference overhead. The adapter can always be re-applied from training checkpoints if needed.

rule.statement: Keep the original base model weights and adapter checkpoints separately. Do not delete adapter checkpoints after merging. If the merged model fails production eval, re-train from the base model — do not attempt to reverse-engineer the adapter from the merged weights.
rule.rationale: Merging is irreversible. Adapter extraction from merged weights produces approximate, lower-quality adapters. The base model + adapter checkpoint is the safe restore point for any rollback scenario.

rule.statement: Deploy fine-tuned models behind an A/B routing layer. Send 5% of traffic to the fine-tuned model; keep 95% on the production baseline. Gate full traffic shift on: P50 latency within 10% of baseline, error rate below 0.1%, domain accuracy above gate threshold from live traffic sample.
rule.rationale: Fine-tuned models can exhibit distribution shift on real production traffic that is not captured in eval datasets. Gradual rollout limits blast radius. The 10% latency gate catches merged model quantization mismatches that increase inference time.

list.item: lora_dropout=0 at inference: PEFT sets dropout to 0 automatically in eval mode; do not manually set it — overriding breaks the saved config
BFloat16 vs Float16: use bf16=True, not fp16=True, for modern GPUs (A100, H100, RTX 3090+); fp16 causes numeric overflow on long sequences
Gradient checkpointing + custom attention: not all models support gradient checkpointing with custom attention implementations; test before long training runs
Max sequence length: set max_seq_length to P99 of your training data length, not the model's maximum; padding to model max wastes VRAM and slows training
PEFT version pinning: PEFT API changes between minor versions; pin to a specific version in requirements and test before upgrades
DeepSpeed ZeRO-3 + PEFT: not all PEFT configs are compatible with ZeRO-3; use ZeRO-2 for multi-GPU LoRA training unless specifically tested

antipattern.description: Training on the full conversation history including previous assistant turns as loss targets.
antipattern.reason: Including previous assistant turns in the loss function teaches the model to predict its own prior outputs, which creates self-reinforcing patterns and increases hallucination. Use DataCollatorForCompletionOnlyLM to mask all tokens except the final assistant response.
